basics.name: Ghostly Gardener
basics.summary: Computer science student at USC interested in the technical and creative side of interactive media.

projects.name: Portal Remake
projects.summary: Game Programming | C++, SDL3, RapidJSON, SDL_Mixer
highlights: Built core gameplay systems for a Portal-inspired 3D first-person puzzle game in C++ using SDL3.
Implemented portal placement and traversal using line-segment casting, quaternions, and matrix transformations, supporting teleportation, camera updates, and momentum remapping.
Built a JSON-driven level loading system to dynamically instantiate actors from configuration files.
Built an audio system with multi-channel playback, asset caching, spatial attenuation, and object binding.

projects.name: Custom 3D Game Engine
highlights: Built a modular 3D game engine in C++ and SDL3 with transforms, camera/projection matrices, depth buffering, textured rendering, materials, and Blinn-Phong lighting.
Implemented a skeletal animation system with keyframe interpolation and animation playback.
Developed collision and debugging modules, including AABB intersection tests, line-segment intersection tests, unit testing, and scoped performance profiling tools.
Extended the rendering pipeline with normal mapping, offscreen render targets, and bloom post-processing.

projects.name: Night Courier
highlights: Designed and developed a 2D top-down stealth game where players scout enemy camps, infer troop direction and numbers from environmental clues, and relay warnings through beacon towers.
Implemented core stealth systems including state-machine-based enemy AI, field-of-view and line-of-sight checks, last-known-position search, NavMesh-based pursuit, and player hiding mechanics.
Built connected-area transitions, dynamic camera-boundary updates with Cinemachine Confiner, and save/load systems for player position and hotbar persistence.

projects.name: A Realm That Drinks Light
projects.summary: Audio Design, Audio Systems Implementation | C#, Unity, Wwise
highlights: Designed the game’s SFX palette, composed original background music, and led technical audio implementation for a 2D side-scrolling exploration-puzzle game in Unity using Wwise and C#.
Built a dynamic environmental audio system using Wwise Events, Switches, and RTPCs, supporting distance-based ambient zones, interactive sound effects, and object-dependent loop control.
Developed a signal-driven player audio system covering footsteps, jump/land, glide, drawing, and ability loops, with RTPC-based speed control and player-state-driven audio transitions.
Established a community of over 150 members, focused on discussions and activities related to the Touhou Project and music.
Organized both offline and online events, including music ensemble sessions, board games, and music composition workshops.
2 years of teaching music theory and aural skills to community members.
